result:
A debouncer delays running the search until the user pauses typing (e.g., 300ms of no keystrokes). Without it, every keystroke fires a request.

Why it matters:
- **Fewer network/API calls** — one request per pause instead of one per character. Lower cost, less load.
- **Avoids race conditions** — stale responses from earlier queries arriving after newer ones.
- **Better UX** — results don't flicker/churn as the user types.
- **Less CPU work** — filtering/rendering large lists only happens when input settles.

Use throttle instead if you want regular updates during continuous input; use debounce when only the final value matters.
